hook.text: Once a write is visible to one client, it's visible to all. No going back. That's linearizability — the gold standard.

buildup.text: Every operation appears to happen at a single, instant point in time. No client sees stale data after a newer read.

discovery.text: Linearizability is needed for distributed locks, leader election, and unique constraint checks.

twist.text: It's expensive. Every write must be acknowledged by a majority of replicas before it's visible.

climax.text: etcd and ZooKeeper provide linearizable reads and writes. They're used as coordination services, not data stores.

punchline.text: Linearizability: once visible, always visible. Essential for coordination.

quiz.question: Why is linearizability needed for distributed locks?
options: All nodes must agree on who holds the lock at any moment to prevent two holders at once
Distributed locks don't require any consistency guarantees
Linearizability makes locks faster to acquire
